model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The schedule/optimizer change already helped, so the next low-complexity lever is modest capacity. A slightly deeper and wider CNN should reduce underfitting on full CIFAR-10 while staying small enough for the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The widened model is now likely benefiting from a little more regularization. A small amount of dropout plus label smoothing is a low-complexity way to tighten validation loss without changing the training budget or optimizer stack.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.05","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The last regularization step hurt validation loss, so the safest move is to restore the stronger no-dropout/no-smoothing baseline and make AdamW slightly more reactive for the short fixed-step horizon. This keeps the larger model and schedule intact while removing the most likely source of regression.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The last change made AdamW too reactive and likely over-regularized the larger network. Backing off weight decay and restoring the slower second-moment setting should recover the better training geometry while keeping the successful architecture and schedule intact.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The model and optimizer are already in a good place, so the remaining slack is likely in how much data each fixed step processes. Doubling the batch size should improve batchnorm stability and let the run cover more CIFAR-10 examples within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last gain came from using the fixed-step budget more aggressively. Doubling the batch again should increase examples seen per step and further stabilize batchnorm, which is a low-complexity way to push validation loss down if the runtime budget still tolerates it.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The batch-size scaling is already paying off, so the next lever is to let the optimizer move a bit faster on each large batch. A modest LR increase is the simplest way to better exploit the 256-step budget without changing the model or schedule shape.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch is already doing the heavy lifting; the last run suggests the step size was a bit too aggressive at this scale. Backing the learning rate down slightly should keep the batch-256 regime stable while leaving room for a lower final loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.01,","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The 1e-3 peak learning rate was still the strongest setting, so the remaining room is in the tail of the schedule. Keeping the higher peak while annealing to a lower floor should help the model settle more cleanly in the last part of the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The step-size and schedule appear close to their best setting, so the remaining lever is model capacity. A modest width increase should help the batch-256 regime fit CIFAR-10 better without changing the overall training loop or adding extra complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 40","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer and batch regime now look settled, so the remaining headroom is likely representational capacity. A moderate width increase should let the model use the large batch more effectively without changing the training loop or introducing extra complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 40","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current run is still improving with added capacity, which suggests the model is not fully saturated yet. Adding one more pooled stage while slightly reducing the starting width should increase representational depth without blowing up compute or parameter count.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now much larger than the initial baseline, and the current run may be under-regularized. Moving weight decay back toward the standard CIFAR-10 setting is a low-cost way to try to tighten validation loss without touching the compute-heavy parts of the recipe.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The stronger weight decay appears to have pushed the model past its best setting. Restoring the lighter regularization and enlarging the classifier head is a low-risk way to let the deeper feature extractor translate into a better final fit.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 40","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 160","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider classifier did not help, but the earlier gains came from more convolutional capacity. Restore the proven head size and shift the extra budget into the feature extractor, where the model is more likely to benefit on CIFAR-10.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 40","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The best validation loss came from the smaller base width, while batch scaling has been the strongest optimizer-side lever so far. Restore the proven convolutional width and try a slightly larger batch to squeeze more data through the fixed step budget without making the network itself more complex.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has been the most reliable lever in this run, and the current recipe still looks data-limited rather than optimizer-limited. A moderate batch increase should raise the number of examples processed under the fixed step budget without changing the proven model shape.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has remained the most reliable improvement path, and the current 384-batch run still looks data-limited rather than capacity-limited. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-scaling trend has been consistently positive, and the current run still looks limited more by data per step than by the model or optimizer recipe.
